key_insight: DrugGen-2 is a disease-aware generative model that combines disease ontology and target protein sequences to design small molecules with improved predicted drug-binding performance.
clinical_relevance: By incorporating disease context into molecule generation, it may help prioritize new or repurposed compounds for more targeted therapeutic discovery.
method: Fine-tuned a pre-trained GPT-2 model on approved drug–disease–target data.
Used a two-step training strategy: supervised fine-tuning followed by GRPO reinforcement learning.
Optimized rewards for chemical validity, novelty, diversity, and predicted binding affinity.
evidence_signal: In evaluation on five diabetic nephropathy-related protein targets, DrugGen-2 outperformed DrugGPT and DrugGen, generating more unique molecules and achieving stronger predicted affinities, with docking hits as low as -9.917 compared with enalapril at -8.283.
watch_item: Results are based on predicted affinity and docking rather than experimental wet-lab validation, so real-world potency and safety remain unconfirmed.
paper_title: DrugGen 2: A disease-aware language model for enhancing drug discovery
